description: How user-provided arguments are injected into command templates. Mechanisms vary: $ARGUMENTS, {{args}}, positional $1/$2/${@:N}, and other interpolation syntaxes.

claude-code.mechanism: $ARGUMENTS (all args), $ARGUMENTS[N] (Nth arg), $N (positional), ${CLAUDE_SESSION_ID} and other env vars; shared with skills argument substitution

cline.mechanism: custom workflow commands (.clinerules/workflows/*.md) are plain prompt templates with no documented argument substitution mechanism

codex.mechanism: no implementation details captured; only source is a redirect stub to external docs — cannot confirm argument substitution support

copilot-cli.mechanism: command file format undocumented; no evidence of argument substitution in plugin.json schema or available sources

gemini-cli.mechanism: {{args}} placeholder injects all user-provided arguments; processed before shell injection (!{...}) and other substitutions; default argument handling collapses newlines

windsurf.mechanism: Windsurf Workflows (.windsurf/workflows/*.md) are plain Markdown prompt templates with no documented argument substitution mechanism
